name: Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – running circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada
region_intro: Jogging around Casarejos offers routes through a landscape characterized by canyons, river valleys, and natural park areas. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the Cañón del río Lobos and routes near the Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew. Elevation gains on many routes indicate a hilly environment, providing a challenging experience for runners. The area's natural features make it suitable for those seeking running paths with scenic views. Best jogging routes around Casarejos The most popular jogging route is Gullurías Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging run through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – running circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada, a difficult 9.0 miles (14.5 km) path. This circular route explores the canyons of Casarejos and the Lobos river. Local runners also love the Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew – Boca del Ocejo loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, a 13.5 miles (21.7 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Jogging around Casarejos is defined by river canyons, natural park landscapes, and routes near historical sites like the Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to advanced running paths. The routes in Casarejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Casarejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Casarejos?
A: There are over 15 dedicated running routes available in the Casarejos area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes traverse diverse terrain, including river canyons and natural park landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy running trails suitable for beginners in Casarejos?
A: While many routes in Casarejos feature challenging terrain with significant elevation changes, there are options that can be considered more moderate. Currently, there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' for jogging, but some trails might be suitable for beginners looking for a moderate challenge. Always check the route details for elevation and distance before you go.

Q: What are some good long-distance running paths in the Casarejos region?
A: For those seeking longer distances, the Casarejos area offers several challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Lobos – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 14.3-mile (22.9 km) trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew – Boca del Ocejo loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos&lt;/a&gt; covers 13.5 miles (21.7 km), both providing extensive running experiences through scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Casarejos?
A: Yes, Casarejos features several excellent circular running routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064201819/canon-de-casarejos-y-canon-del-rio-lobos-running-circular-desde-casarejos-por-el-barranco-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – running circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 9.0-mile (14.5 km) path that explores the stunning canyons of Casarejos and the Lobos river.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the running trails around Casarejos?
A: Runners in Casarejos can expect breathtaking views of river canyons, natural park areas, and diverse terrain. Many routes offer scenic vistas, such as those along the Cañón del río Lobos. You might also encounter viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic perspectives of the landscape.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while running in Casarejos?
A: The running routes in Casarejos often pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You can explore areas near the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt;, or discover natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Grande (Río Lobos Canyon)&lt;/a&gt;. The region's trails also lead through the Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, offering a rich experience.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Casarejos?
A: The running trails in Casarejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the challenging nature of the routes, and the stunning natural beauty of the canyons and river valleys.

Q: Are there any advanced running paths for experienced runners in Casarejos?
A: Yes, Casarejos is well-suited for advanced runners. Many of the routes are graded as 'difficult' due to significant elevation gains and varied terrain. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064198016/gullurias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.4-mile (10.3 km) run, offer a challenging experience through the region's hilly environment.

Q: Is Casarejos a dog-friendly area for running?
A: While the guide focuses on running routes, the natural park areas around Casarejos, such as the Cañón del Río Lobos, generally welcome dogs. However, it's always recommended to check specific park regulations or individual trail rules regarding leashes and waste disposal before heading out with your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Casarejos?
A: The Casarejos region, with its canyons and natural park areas, can be enjoyed for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winters might present cooler conditions, especially in shaded canyon areas.

Q: Are there any hidden gem running trails near Casarejos?
A: The Casarejos area is rich with natural beauty, and many trails offer a sense of discovery. While specific 'hidden gems' aren't explicitly listed, exploring the less-trafficked sections of the Cañón del río Lobos or trails leading to lesser-known highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5939096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colmenar de los Frailes&lt;/a&gt; can provide a unique and rewarding running experience away from the most popular spots.
tips: This park is known for its spectacular geological formations, caves and chasms, as well as its rich biodiversity.
Near the Lobos river and next to the hermitage of San Bartolomé, we will find this spectacular cave, known as Cueva Grande, from where it is typical to take a spectacular photo of the hermitage.

Located in a unique setting, isolated at the mouth of the Lobos River Canyon, this beautiful construction was part of a Templar monastery, San Juan de Otero, of which only the chapel remains. The architectural symbols inscribed on its walls have been the source of numerous debates, attributing some to them of esoteric origin.

waypoints.name: View of the Hermitage of San Bartolomé from Cueva Grande
